description: Launched and scaled a suite of evergreen and semi-liquid private markets fund products targeting the wealth channel, including the Hamilton Lane Private Assets Fund (an interval fund) and multiple perpetual-vehicle structures across PE, credit, and infrastructure. These vehicles offer quarterly liquidity windows with lower minimums than traditional drawdown funds, designed to make private markets accessible to high-net-worth individuals and retail platforms.
stated_rationale: Capture the secular shift of wealth-channel capital into private markets by offering semi-liquid, lower-minimum structures that reduce the J-curve effect and operational complexity for non-institutional investors. Position Hamilton Lane as a first-mover in democratized private-markets access alongside Partners Group and iCapital.
observable_metric_impact: Evergreen/perpetual-vehicle AUM grew substantially from 2022 to 2025, contributing to overall AUM growth from ~$124B to ~$146B. The Private Assets Fund attracted significant inflows through wirehouse and RIA distribution partnerships. Evergreen structures improved fee stability by reducing the fundraising cyclicality inherent in traditional drawdown vehicles. Management fee rates on evergreen products generally exceeded those on customized SMAs.
op_prereq_summary: Required regulatory structuring under the Investment Company Act of 1940 for interval fund registration; demanded new distribution agreements with wirehouses (e.g., Morgan Stanley, UBS, Merrill Lynch) and RIA platforms; necessitated building a dedicated wealth-channel sales team and investor-education infrastructure.

description: Expanded the secondaries and co-investment direct investment platform, growing direct investment AUM and increasing the volume of co-investment and secondary transaction flow sourced through Hamilton Lane's proprietary deal network. The firm leveraged its position as one of the largest private-markets allocators globally (~$929B in assets under management and supervision) to source differentiated secondary and co-investment opportunities that smaller platforms cannot access.
stated_rationale: Monetize Hamilton Lane's structural information advantage — derived from managing and advising on nearly $1T in private-markets assets — by deploying capital directly into secondaries and co-investments at favorable terms. These strategies carry higher fee rates than advisory/SMA mandates and align with LP demand for more active portfolio management and liquidity solutions.
observable_metric_impact: Specialized fund AUM (including secondaries and co-investment vehicles) grew as a share of total AUM through 2023-2025. Hamilton Lane raised successive dedicated secondaries funds and co-investment vehicles, with fund sizes increasing across vintages. The direct investment platform contributed to management fee growth outpacing AUM growth, reflecting the higher-fee-rate mix shift. Reported deploying over $10B in secondary and co-investment transactions annually by 2024.
op_prereq_summary: Required scaling the dedicated direct-investment team with experienced secondaries and co-investment professionals; demanded development of proprietary deal-flow technology to filter and evaluate thousands of secondary market opportunities annually; leveraged existing SMA relationships as a sourcing funnel for co-investment deal flow.

description: Continued investment in and commercialization of the proprietary data and analytics technology platform, including the Cobalt LP portfolio monitoring tool and the broader Hamilton Lane data ecosystem covering 50,000+ private-markets funds. The firm positioned its technology stack as both an internal competitive advantage for underwriting and portfolio construction and an external revenue-generating product sold to institutional LPs, consultants, and wealth platforms.
stated_rationale: Differentiate Hamilton Lane from competing private-markets advisors and fund managers through a data and technology moat that is difficult to replicate without similar scale. The analytics platform deepens client stickiness by embedding Hamilton Lane's tools into LP workflows, creating switching costs that extend beyond any single fund commitment cycle.
observable_metric_impact: Technology and data analytics revenue grew as a distinct line item, contributing to fee-related earnings diversification. The Cobalt platform's client base expanded across institutional investors and consultants globally. The proprietary database — covering performance, cash flows, and valuations across 50,000+ funds — informed Hamilton Lane's own fund selection and underwriting, supporting above-benchmark returns in SMA programs. Technology investment also supported operational scalability, enabling AUM-per-employee ratios to improve through 2023-2025.
op_prereq_summary: Required sustained R&D investment in software engineering and data science teams; demanded integration of data ingestion pipelines across thousands of GP reporting formats; necessitated commercial licensing agreements with institutional clients and careful management of data confidentiality obligations across LP and GP relationships.
